title: Lola
html:
Lola

The family dog. Lola crosses three notes spanning July 2022 through May 2024, and her arc — from being washed before a Vietnam trip to being prepared for a "farewell" two years later — is one of the quietest grief-threads in the corpus. She is rarely the subject of any note, but she is present in the background of major Yahir transitions, and the way she's referenced shifts from logistical to commemorative as her life ends.

The earliest mention is in 2022 07 31 vietnam route prep — "wash Lola" sits in the same to-do list as the Ha Giang Bike Tour, the Phong Nha caves, the Vietnam visa, and a CS161B coursework reminder. Pre-trip dog-washing is the kind of detail that anchors Lola to the Grants Pass family-house backdrop: she's part of the home Yahir is leaving for the summer. By the December 2022 Christmas Eve note (2022 12 25 christmas eve 2022) — the dad-psychosis episode where Valeria slaps her father and Yahir spits at him — Lola is witnessing. She doesn't act in the scene, but the note specifically places her there. The most acute family conflict in the surfaced corpus has Lola in the room.

The May 2024 todos (2024 05 13 things) close the arc: "Call vet for lola farewell" and "Print out lola picture" sit alongside the Shanghai visa scramble, the India/Nepal backpack prep, and the Ford scholarship admin. The vet-call-plus-printed-picture combination is end-of-life ritual: she was likely put down right before Yahir left for China. The China chapter — the longest stretch he ever spent away from home — begins with Lola's death as the parting wound. The fact that he encoded the picture-printing as a discrete to-do item alongside everything else is itself the proof: he was making a small, deliberate keepsake before leaving the country for six months. The grief is not narrated anywhere in the surfaced corpus, but the shape of it is visible in those two task lines.

  • 2022 12 25 christmas eve 2022
  • 2024 05 13 things
